content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 12, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 12, 2026, focused on stabilizing the Eliza Cloud infrastructure. Key achievements include finalizing the app-node ingress configuration with Caddy and improving control-plane reliability through automated Headscale installation.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cloud Infrastructure & Ingress**
*   Completed the app-node cloud-init configuration by installing Caddy as the front-door ingress, replacing the final TODO in the deployment pipeline ([#8394](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8394)).
*   Streamlined control-plane provisioning by moving Headscale installation to the cloud-init bootstrap process, ensuring consistent systemd unit and state directory management ([#8393](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8393)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8399](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8399): Dependency update for `esbuild` (0.28.0 to 0.28.1) in the cloud-services operator.
*   [#8396](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8396): Dependency update for `pypdf` (6.10.2 to 6.12.0) in the OSWorld benchmarks package.
*   [#8395](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8395): Added EvoLink support to the `plugin-openai` package.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**[#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321) Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live**
*   **Status:** The infrastructure is largely verified on staging, with the core design (tenant DB isolation, auto-TLS, and container billing) confirmed as functional.
*   **Progress:** Recent efforts successfully adopted shared resources into the `apps-shared` module without data loss. The app-node is now running the new Caddy configuration.
*   **Challenges:** The primary remaining blockers for a full end-to-end go-live are a `join_mode` configuration update for the staging tenant and the final arming of the build-from-repo pipeline. Developers are coordinating on these final steps to ensure the provisioning worker remains stable during the transition.
